businessType: Cyclical / capital-intensive semiconductor memory (DRAM + NAND + HBM)
summary: MU is the primary Western beneficiary of the AI memory supercycle with HBM sold out through 2026, but at $462 the stock trades 23% above base-case fair value. Thesis intact, but entry timing is stretched — wait for a pullback into the $350-400 range.
thesisStatement: Micron is the primary Western beneficiary of an AI-driven memory supercycle. HBM demand structurally lifts earnings power above historical mid-cycle levels, while tight industry supply discipline sustains pricing longer than in previous memory cycles.
thesisBullets: HBM sold out through calendar 2026 with binding agreements
Cloud Memory revenue doubled YoY to $5.3B in FQ1 (39% of revenue)
DRAM/NAND prices surged ~90% QoQ in early 2026
FQ2 guidance implies 68% gross margin — unprecedented for Micron
Capex rising to $20B in FY2026 to support HBM capacity
variantPerception: The market is pricing MU as though HBM has permanently cured memory cyclicality. It probably hasn't. At ~14x FY2026 consensus EPS of $33, MU looks optically cheap, but P/E looks cheapest at cyclical peaks. Conventional DRAM and NAND (still 60%+ of revenue) remain commodity markets that will normalize. A 68% gross margin is not sustainable — 45-50% is a more realistic mid-cycle with HBM structural uplift.

valuationLens.crossCheck: EV/EBIT on normalized operating income (~12.4x on FY26E peak)
valuationLens.rationale: Standard cyclical framework. For memory, the key is estimating sustainable mid-cycle margins, not peak-quarter margins. FCF yield is a poor cross-check because $20B capex distorts near-term free cash flow.

currentValuationSnapshot.balanceSheetNote: Net debt approximately zero ($11.8B debt vs $12B cash). Balance sheet healthy, but $20B capex (~27% of FY26 revenue) reflects capital intensity.

newsToModel.event: FQ2 guided revenue $18.7B (+37% QoQ) with 68% gross margin
newsToModel.impact: Pushes FY26 EPS consensus above $33; validates near-term AI demand acceleration

newsToModel.event: HBM supply sold out through CY2026 with binding agreements
newsToModel.impact: Reduces near-term downside risk; HBM revenue run-rate ~$8B annualized

newsToModel.event: DRAM/NAND prices jumped 90% QoQ in Q1 2026
newsToModel.impact: Extraordinary price spike raises peak earnings but raises sustainability question
newsToModel.affectedScenario: Bull short-term, Bear medium-term — extreme price moves often precede reversals

newsToModel.event: Applied Materials partnership for next-gen DRAM/HBM/NAND co-development
newsToModel.impact: Modest positive for maintaining technology edge; no near-term P&L impact

newsToModel.event: HBM market share: Micron ~11-21%, SK Hynix ~53-62%, Samsung recovering
newsToModel.impact: Micron is smallest HBM player; share gains possible but Samsung catching up is a risk
newsToModel.affectedScenario: Bear — Samsung recovery pressures pricing; Bull — Micron gains toward 25%

newsToModel.impact: Higher capex depresses near-term FCF and increases cyclical risk if downturn arrives mid-build

scenarios.label: Bear
scenarios.operatingAssumption: FY2027: Memory cycle peaks mid-FY26 and normalizes. Revenue $55B, gross margin 38% as conventional DRAM/NAND reprices down while HBM mix limits the trough.
scenarios.valuationAssumption: 14x forward P/E — early recovery pricing for a cyclical at trough earnings.
scenarios.whatMustBeTrue: DRAM/NAND supply catches up as all three makers spend heavily. Samsung HBM4 catches up and pressures HBM pricing. AI capex growth decelerates. 90% QoQ price increases prove to be the cycle peak.

scenarios.label: Base
scenarios.operatingAssumption: FY2027: HBM structurally lifts mid-cycle. Revenue $70B, gross margin 48% as HBM sustains premium pricing but conventional memory normalizes from peak.
scenarios.valuationAssumption: 18x P/E — premium over historical memory multiples reflecting structural HBM growth layer.
scenarios.whatMustBeTrue: AI capex continues at current or higher levels through 2027. HBM remains supply-constrained with 3 disciplined suppliers. Conventional DRAM/NAND reprices down 20-30% from peak but doesn't collapse.

scenarios.label: Bull
scenarios.operatingAssumption: FY2027: AI supercycle extends, HBM TAM toward $80-100B. Revenue $90B, gross margin 55% with HBM revenue reaching $20B+.
scenarios.valuationAssumption: 20x P/E — reflects sustained structural growth and pricing power in AI memory.
scenarios.whatMustBeTrue: HBM TAM grows to $80B+ by 2027. Micron gains share to 20-25%. AI capex accelerates. Memory supply discipline holds despite massive industry capex.

currentPriceImplies: At $462, the market is pricing in a scenario between base and bull — that HBM has permanently elevated Micron's earnings power to ~$25-30 sustainable EPS and that a conventional memory downturn either won't happen or will be shallow. At 18x sustainable EPS, the stock implies ~$25.70 in ongoing earnings, well above the base case of $20.70. The market has fully priced out a normal cyclical downturn (198% premium to bear) and requires HBM to over-deliver to justify the current price (23% premium to base). The key risk: if gross margins normalize to 45% instead of 50-55%, EPS drops to $18-22 and the stock needs to re-rate significantly lower.
currentPriceImpliesBrief: Market prices in sustained $25-30 EPS — between base and bull, fully pricing out a normal memory cycle downturn.

currentPriceImpliedFacts.label: Implied Gross Margin
currentPriceImpliedFacts.value: ~50-55% on $70-75B revenue — above historical mid-cycle, below current 68%

currentPriceImpliedFacts.label: Premium to Bear Case
currentPriceImpliedFacts.value: +198% — market has fully priced out cyclical downturn

provisionalConclusion: Fair, but at the upper edge — closer to slightly rich for a cyclical. MU at $462 sits 23% above base-case fair value of $375 and requires a scenario between base and bull to justify. Paying 14x peak earnings for a cyclical memory company assumes a margin of safety that doesn't exist. For a new position: wait for a pullback into the $350-400 range. For existing holders: thesis intact, but adding at $462 carries meaningful cyclical risk.
technicalCommentary: MU has rallied 21.6% in 2 weeks and +49% YTD, trading at all-time highs ahead of FQ2 earnings. The dominant pattern is a momentum chase into earnings — no technical washout or mean-reversion setup exists. Even if FQ2 beats, the stock may sell the news if guidance doesn't accelerate further.
risks: Memory cycle peak: 68% GM and 90% QoQ price increases are historically unsustainable
Samsung HBM4 catch-up could erode Micron's HBM pricing power
AI capex deceleration by hyperscalers would reduce HBM demand outlook
$20B capex creates stranded capacity risk if downturn arrives mid-build
Micron is smallest HBM player (~11-21% share) — vulnerable to competitive share shifts
Conventional DRAM/NAND oversupply as all three makers expand capacity simultaneously
catalysts: FQ2 results tonight (March 18) — beat + strong FQ3 guide could push stock higher
HBM4 ramp in Q2 CY2026 with high yields — technology leadership confirmation
HBM TAM expansion from $35B to $100B by 2028 if AI capex accelerates
Market share gains in HBM toward 20-25% from current 11-21%
Applied Materials partnership improving next-gen technology cost structure
